title: Structural Format Validators (JSON/Markdown/XML)
goal: Implement deterministic structural validators in src/inspector.py for JSON, Markdown, and XML output formats. Each validator parses the model response and returns a pass/fail result with detail.

in_scope: Implement validate_json(text) using json.loads — report valid/invalid with parse error detail
Implement validate_markdown(text) checking basic heading/list structure presence
Implement validate_xml(text) using xml.etree.ElementTree — report parseable/invalid with error detail
Return consistent result dict: {check_name, status, detail}
Handle empty/None inputs gracefully with 'fail' status and descriptive detail
All validators run locally with zero network dependency
out_of_scope: Deep Markdown structure analysis (link validation, image checks)

inputs: Model response text (string) from inference results
PRD Week 2 section 4 — Output format validation
outputs: src/inspector.py — validate_json(), validate_markdown(), validate_xml() fully implemented
checklist: Implement validate_json(text): attempt json.loads(text), return pass on success
On json.loads failure, capture JSONDecodeError message and return fail with error detail
Implement validate_markdown(text): check for presence of heading (# ) and/or list (- or *) markers
Markdown validation returns pass if at least one structural element is found, fail otherwise
Include detail listing which structural elements were found or missing
Implement validate_xml(text): attempt ET.fromstring(text), return pass on success
On XML parse failure, capture ParseError message and return fail with error detail
Handle None input in all validators: return fail with 'No output to validate'
Handle empty string input in all validators: return fail with 'Empty output'
Ensure returned dict always has keys: check_name (str), status ('pass'|'fail'), detail (str)
acceptance_criteria: validate_json('not json') returns status='fail' with JSON parse error in detail
validate_xml('not xml') returns status='fail' with XML parse error in detail
validate_markdown('plain text with no structure') returns status='fail'
All validators handle None and empty string inputs without raising exceptions
No network calls are made during any validation
review_focus: JSON validator uses standard library json.loads (no external dependencies)
XML validator uses standard library xml.etree.ElementTree (no external dependencies)
Markdown heuristic is reasonable — checks for headings and/or list markers without being overly strict
Error detail messages are informative but don't expose internal paths or stack traces
Result dict structure is identical across all validators
risks_or_notes: Markdown validation is heuristic-based and may produce false positives/negatives for edge cases
JSON/XML validators may need to handle responses that contain structured content embedded within prose (addressed in Part 03's no-extra-text check)
Very large model responses may have performance implications for XML parsing — unlikely but worth noting
Consider whether to strip leading/trailing whitespace before validation
definition_of_done: All three validators are fully implemented in src/inspector.py
Each returns consistent {check_name, status, detail} dicts
Edge cases (None, empty, invalid) are handled gracefully
No external dependencies beyond Python standard library
